FALK, BUXTON & BROWN 
Harry W. Falk (1889-1972) 
Donald J. Falk (1919-1970) 
Charles E. Buxton (1928-1980) 
J. Michael Brown 
James B. Bartlett 
Stella Connell Levy 
Staff Counsel 
Attorneys-at-Law 
>", 
937 SIXTH STREET -
P. O. BOX J334 
EUREKA, '-=~.U''-' 
Hay 19, 1981 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Telephone 
442-5701 
Area Code 707 
Re: 
Humboldt County Planning Commission 
Dear Stella: 
Thank you very much for the recent opinions you forwarded 
to me. 
I also want to thank you for taking the time to talk to 
me on the telephone regarding my possible conflicts of interest 
in ruling on local coastal programs as a part of my duties as a 
Humboldt County Planning Conrr:lissioner. 
There is one point that I did want to confirm with you 
having to do with those local coastal programs. 
During our 
discussion I stated that I may have a conflict on one or two 
aspects within a local coastal plan and would therefore abstain 
from voting regarding those issues. 
I then asked whether I 
could vote on the plan as a whole once those issues had been 
decided and you advised that I could as I would not have a conflict 
as to the plan as a whole. 
I of course agree with that statement 
in light of the fact that my conflicts, if any, are of a very 
minor nature and probably don't meet the tests as set forth in 
the administrative code anyway. 
I felt it best, however, to 
confirm this one point with you as it might appear peculiar to 
the public that I'abstain from voting on'a specific issue within 
a plan and then vote on the plan as a whole. 
If I have misinterpreted your remarks in any manner I 
would appreciate your dropping me a note to that effect as soon 
as you can as we will be voting on the Eel River Land Use Plan, 
as a part of the Eel River Local Coastal Program in the near 
future. 
Very truly you~s, 
J. Michael Bro~tJTI 
Jl1B:mdr

sections.question_synthetic: Can a Humboldt County Planning Commissioner abstain from voting on specific conflicted issues within a local coastal plan and then vote on the plan as a whole, and does this create an appearance of impropriety?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request/correspondence letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. The document references prior FPPC opinions and a telephone discussion but does not contain a formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section. The writer is seeking confirmation of informal guidance previously provided by FPPC staff. Document quality is poor with OCR artifacts and formatting issues affecting readability.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can a Humboldt County Planning Commissioner abstain from voting on specific conflicted issues within a local coastal plan and then vote on the plan as a whole, and does this create an appearance of impropriety?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming letter from attorney J. Michael Brown confirming details of a prior telephone conversation with FPPC staff counsel Stella Connell Levy regarding conflicts of interest for his service on the Humboldt County Planning Commission, specifically regarding local coastal program votes.
